meaning: adj.妨碍活动的n.使受挫折的事物
examples: High marginal tax rates may act as a disincentive to working longer hours.
That implicit marginal tax rate of 23 percent is a significant disincentive.
A plunging share price may be the biggest disincentive to the corrupters of the weak and greedy.
He spoke of lowering the corporate tax rate--now a challenging disincentive to U.S. companies.
This too is a disincentive to work and undermines one ostensible purpose of the Fed's easing.
